chapter_title.en: Chapter 10 – The names and the teknonyms

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/33)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (1/33)
text: اسْتَعْمَلَ مُعَاوِيَةُ مَرْوَانَ بْنَ الْحَكَمِ عَلَى الْمَدِينَةِ وَ أَمَرَهُ أَنْ يَفْرِضَ لِشَباب قُرَيْشٍ فَفَرَضَ لَهُمْ فَقَالَ عَلِيُّ بْنُ الْحُسَيْنِ ( عليه السلام ) فَأَتَيْتُهُ فَقَالَ مَا اسْمُكَ فَقُلْتُ عَلِيُّ بْنُ الْحُسَيْنِ فَقَالَ مَا اسْمُ أَخِيكَ فَقُلْتُ عَلِيٌّ قَالَ عَلِيٌّ وَ عَلِيٌّ مَا يُرِيدُ أَبُوكَ أَنْ يَدَعَ أَحَداً مِنْ وُلْدِهِ إِلَّا سَمَّاهُ عَلِيّاً

ثُمَّ فَرَضَ لِي فَرَجَعْتُ إِلَى أَبِي فَأَخْبَرْتُهُ فَقَالَ وَيْلِي عَلَى ابْنِ الزَّرْقَاءِ دَبَّاغَةِ الْأَدَمِ لَوْ وُلِدَ لِي مِائَةٌ لَأَحْبَبْتُ أَنْ لَا أُسَمِّيَ أَحَداً مِنْهُمْ إِلَّا عَلِيّاً .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Al Barqy, from Abdul Rahman Bin Muhammad Al Azramy who said,

‘Muawiya (appointed) Marwan Bin Al-Hakam as an office bearer at Al-Medina, and instructed him that he should impose upon the youth of Qureysh, so he imposed to them. So Ali<sup>asws</sup> Bin Al-Husayn<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘So he came over to me<sup>asws</sup>, so he said, ‘What is your<sup>asws</sup> name?’ So I said, ‘Ali<sup>asws</sup> Bin Al-Husayn<sup>asws</sup>’. So he said, ‘What is the name of your<sup>asws</sup> brother?’ So I<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Ali’. So he said, ‘Ali and Ali, what did your<sup>asws</sup> father<sup>asws</sup> intend, that he<sup>asws</sup> did not leave anyone from his<sup>asws</sup> sons except that he<sup>asws</sup> named him as Ali?’

Then he imposed to me. So I<sup>asws</sup> returned to my<sup>asws</sup> father<sup>asws</sup>, so I<sup>asws</sup> informed him<sup>asws</sup>. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Woe be unto the son of Zarqa, the tanner of the unseeded land. If there were born for me<sup>asws</sup> one hundred (sons), I<sup>asws</sup> would love it not to name anyone of them except as Ali’.
en.sarwar: 7. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad formal-Barqiy from ‘Abd al-Rahman ibn Muhammad al-‘Arzamiy who has said the following: “Mu‘awiyah appointed Marwan ibn al-Hakam as his agent in al-Madinah and ordered him to pay a certain salary to the young people of Quraysh which he did. Ali ibn al-Husayn, ‘Alayhim al-Salam, has said, ‘I went to him and he asked, ‘What is your name?’ I replied, ‘It is Ali ibn al-Husayn.’ He then asked, ‘What is the name of your brother?’ I replied, ‘It is Ali.’ He said, ‘Ali and Ali. Is it that your father does not want to leave any of his children without naming them all Ali?’ He paid me a certain amount. I returned to my father and informed him of what had happened. He (the Imam) said, ‘Woe is upon the son of al-Zarqa’, the leather- treating man. Even if one hundred sons will be born to me I will not give anyone of them any other name except Ali.’”
